title: ‘Constructive’ talks, destructive reality as Kyiv endures relentless Russian drones | DW News
description:
A massive power outage hit Ukraine Saturday, plunging the capital Kyiv and several regions into darkness for hours. The blackout was caused by a technical malfunction on transmission lines connecting Ukraine, Moldova, and Romania. It's another blow to a country already struggling with weeks of Russian strikes on its energy infrastructure during one of the coldest winters in years. Ukraine and Russia are set to hold direct talks in Abu Dhabi today aimed at exploring a possible end to the four-year-long war. With analysis from DW's Russia analyst Konstantin Eggert.

#russiaukrainewar #kyiv #abudhabi

title: Russia, Ukraine halt energy attacks during cold spell | DW News
description:
Russia and Ukraine say they've halted strikes on each other's energy infrastructure after a request from the White House. Ukrainian President Volodymr Zelenksyy says the energy truce is supposed to last a week, but the Kremlin says it will end on Sunday. Russia has since switched its focus, and continued attacks on other key infrastructure. 

00:00 Russia, Ukraine hold energy attacks during cold spell
02:18 Konstantin Eggert, DW Russia Analyst
03:25 Marianna Fakhurdinova, Center for European Policy Analysis

title: How powerful is Russia really? | To the Point
description:
Nico Lange, a Military Expert on Russia's war in Ukraine: 'this is not a superpower waging war, it's smallscale infantry fighting'.

title: Iran has warned that it will respond 'instantly' if attacked | DW News
description:
President Trump has warned Tehran that it will face another military attack if it does not accept a new nuclear deal. Trump is sending massive firepower to the Middle East and says that any attack on Iran would be much worse than those carried out by the U.S. last year. 

Chapters:
0:00 EU and US turn up pressure on Iran
0:25 Giorgio Cafiero, CEO of Gulf State Analytics
